steps.title: Recognize the prepositional phrase structure
steps.content: The blank follows “with the even older Hereford map…” This creates a prepositional phrase. Since a prepositional phrase begins with a preposition followed by a noun phrase, any verb in the phrase must be non-finite—it cannot be the main verb of a clause.

steps.title: Apply the verb-form rule
steps.content: Being is a participle (a non-finite verb form) that correctly completes the prepositional phrase: “with the Hereford map being another one.” The options is, has been, and was are finite verbs that cannot serve as non-finite modifiers in a prepositional phrase. That matches choice A — A is correct.
